from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Generate a single fluent sentence that summarizes all provided triples."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping from predicate to a short verbal phrase.
    # The lambda receives (subject, object) and returns a clause fragment.
    phrase_map = {
        "location": lambda s, o: f"is located in {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"is in {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"has capital {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"has demonym \"{o}\"",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"is led by {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"has leader title {o}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"uses the currency {o}",
        "architect": lambda s, o: f"was designed by {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"is owned by {o}",
        "developer": lambda s, o: f"was developed by {o}",
        "tenant": lambda s, o: f"has tenant {o}",
        "currentTenants": lambda s, o: f"currently houses {o}",
        "floorCount": lambda s, o: f"has {o} floors",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"is {o} tall",
        "cost": lambda s, o: f"costs {o}",
        "floorArea": lambda s, o: f"covers a floor area of {o}",
        "buildingStartDate": lambda s, o: f"started construction in {o}",
        "completionDate": lambda s, o: f"was completed in {o}",
        "inaugurationDate": lambda s, o: f"was inaugurated on {o}",
        "yearOfConstruction": lambda s, o: f"was built in {o}",
        "architecturalStyle": lambda s, o: f"is designed in the {o} style",
        "buildingType": lambda s, o: f"is a {o}",
        "address": lambda s, o: f"is located at {o}",
        "postalCode": lambda s, o: f"has postal code {o}",
        "region": lambda s, o: f"is in the region {o}",
        "state": lambda s, o: f"is in the state {o}",
        "website": lambda s, o: f"has website {o}",
        "significantBuilding": lambda s, o: f"is known for the building {o}",
        "significantProject": lambda s, o: f"is known for the project {o}",
        "origin": lambda s, o: f"originates from {o}",
        "foundationPlace": lambda s, o: f"was founded in {o}",
        "keyPerson": lambda s, o: f"has key person {o}",
        "chancellor": lambda s, o: f"is headed by chancellor {o}",
        "governingBody": lambda s, o: f"is governed by {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"is led by {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"is part of {o}",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": lambda s, o: f"was added to the National Register of Historic Places on {o}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": lambda s, o: f"has NRHP reference number {o}",
        "bedCount": lambda s, o: f"has {o} beds",
        "numberOfRooms": lambda s, o: f"contains {o} rooms",
        # default fallback
        "default": lambda s, o: f"{s} {s.replace('_', ' ')} {o}",
    }

    # Group clauses by subject
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_clauses = defaultdict(list)

    for triple in triples:
        pred = triple.predicate
        obj = triple.object
        phrase_func = phrase_map.get(pred, phrase_map["default"])
        clause = phrase_func(triple.subject, obj)
        subject_clauses[triple.subject].append(clause)

    # Build sentence fragments for each subject
    parts = []
    for subj, clauses in subject_clauses.items():
        if len(clauses) == 1:
            parts.append(f"{subj} {clauses[0]}.")
        else:
            # join with commas and 'and' before last
            body = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + f", and {clauses[-1]}"
            parts.append(f"{subj} {body}.")

    # Combine all subject parts into one coherent paragraph
    sentence = " ".join(parts)
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

Change 1: Replace:
  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """Generate a single fluent sentence that summarizes all provided triples."""
      if not triples:
          return ""
      # Use the subject of the first triple as the main subject of the sentence
      main_subject = triples[0].subject
      # Build predicate‑object fragments
      fragments = []
      for triple in triples:
          # Make predicate more readable by replacing underscores with spaces
          pred = triple.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
          fragments.append(f"{pred} {triple.object}")
      # Assemble the final sentence
      if len(fragments) == 1:
          sentence = f"The {fragments[0]} of {main_subject}."
      else:
          # Join all but the last fragment with commas, add 'and' before the final one
          body = ", ".join(fragments[:-1]) + f", and {fragments[-1]}"
          sentence = f"{main_subject} has {body}."
      return sentence
